Title: OsLESV and OsESV1 promote transitory and storage starch biosynthesis to determine rice grain quality and yield

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Leaf transitory starch serves as a carbon and energy reserve; endosperm storage starch determines grain quality and yield
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf chloroplast starch granules; endosperm amyloplast starch granules
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of both leaf transitory starch and endosperm storage starch; loss of function reduces total starch and amylose, increases endosperm chalkiness, and lowers grain weight and yield
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant mapping; complementation with native-promoter genomic construct; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; double mutant analysis; iodine staining; TEM/SEM; starch and amylose quantification; subcellular localization; starch-binding assay; Y2H; BiFC; GST pull-down; cell-free degradation assay; granule-bound protein assay; overexpression rescue of downstream genes
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Non-enzymatic starch-granule-binding protein with chloroplast transit peptide and C-terminal tryptophan-rich starch-binding domain
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast; predominantly starch granule-associated insoluble fraction at end of day
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in all examined tissues; higher in leaves and young endosperms; diurnally regulated, decreasing during day and increasing at night under carbon starvation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in all examined tissues; higher in leaves and young endosperms; induced by carbon starvation with night-upregulated expression
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsLESV promoter for complementation; maize Ubiquitin1 promoter used for downstream-gene overexpression rescue assays in mutants
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome analysis of flag leaves; accession NA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsLESV was identified by map-based cloning of the lsd1 mutant and confirmed by complementation as a determinant of leaf and endosperm starch accumulation. 2. OsLESV binds starch through a conserved C-terminal Trp-rich region and localizes to chloroplast starch granules. 3. OsLESV physically interacts with GBSSI, GBSSII, and PPDKB, maintains their protein stability and enzyme activity, and facilitates targeting of GBSSI and GBSSII to starch granules. 4. Loss of OsLESV impairs grain quality and yield, while overexpression of GBSSI, GBSSII, or PPDKB partially rescues the mutant phenotypes.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Day/night cycle; carbon starvation status inferred from diurnal starch turnover
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes reduced plant height, seed setting rate, grain thickness, 1000-grain weight, and grain yield; altered endosperm starch physicochemical properties
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving rice grain quality and yield by enhancing starch accumulation; could be combined with GBSSI/GBSSII/PPDKB-based engineering or allele improvement to optimize source-sink starch metabolism
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of key starch biosynthetic enzymes but is not itself a catalytic enzyme or transcription factor; also known as LSD1; likely mediates enzyme targeting to starch granules in addition to stabilizing proteins

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Leaf transitory starch serves as a carbon and energy reserve; endosperm storage starch determines grain quality and yield
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf chloroplast starch granules; endosperm amyloplast starch granules
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes transitory starch in leaves and storage starch in endosperm; mutation reduces starch and amylose, elevates sucrose, worsens grain chalkiness, and reduces grain yield
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; single and double mutant analysis; iodine staining; TEM/SEM; starch and amylose quantification; subcellular localization; starch-binding assay; Y2H; BiFC; GST pull-down; cell-free degradation assay; granule-bound protein assay; overexpression rescue of downstream genes
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Non-enzymatic starch-granule-binding protein with chloroplast transit peptide and C-terminal tryptophan-rich starch-binding domain
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast; starch granule-associated insoluble fraction, especially at end of day
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in examined tissues, higher in leaves; diurnally regulated with lower daytime and higher nighttime expression; induced by carbon starvation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in examined tissues, higher in leaves; induced by carbon starvation and follows diurnal rhythm
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: maize Ubiquitin1 promoter used for downstream-gene overexpression rescue assays in mutants
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome analysis of flag leaves; accession NA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsESV1 is a rice homolog of Arabidopsis ESV1 required for normal accumulation of transitory starch in leaves and storage starch in endosperm. 2. OsESV1 binds starch via its conserved C-terminal Trp-rich region and localizes to chloroplast starch granules. 3. OsESV1 interacts with GBSSI, GBSSII, and PPDKB to maintain protein stability and enzyme activity, and is especially important for targeting GBSSII to starch granules. 4. Loss of OsESV1 compromises grain quality, yield, and plant architecture, and some defects can be partially rescued by overexpressing GBSSI, GBSSII, or PPDKB.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Day/night cycle; carbon starvation status inferred from diurnal starch turnover
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes reduced grain thickness, 1000-grain weight, grain yield, plant height, seed setting rate, and loose plant architecture with larger tiller angle
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to improve starch accumulation, grain appearance quality, and yield through manipulation of starch-granule-associated regulatory proteins
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also named LSD2 in this study; partially overlaps functionally with OsLESV but has a stronger described role in targeting GBSSII to starch granules

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycosyltransferase; starch synthase family; granule-bound starch synthase
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: oslesv-1 and osesv1-1 mutant backgrounds in Nipponbare
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage carbohydrate determining grain quality and yield
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Amylose glucan chains in starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes amylose and total starch accumulation in endosperm and contributes to grain quality; overexpression partially rescues oslesv and osesv1 defects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction assays with OsLESV/OsESV1 (Y2H, BiFC, GST pull-down); immunoblot abundance analysis; enzyme activity assay; granule-bound protein fraction analysis; overexpression rescue in oslesv and osesv1 backgrounds; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid/chloroplast or amyloplast starch granule; insoluble granule-bound fraction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in developing endosperm; abundance reduced in oslesv/osesv1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq context for starch-metabolism genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for amylose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GBSSI physically interacts with OsLESV and OsESV1. 2. Its transcript level, protein abundance, enzyme activity, and starch-granule association are reduced in oslesv and osesv1 mutants. 3. Overexpression of GBSSI partially restores leaf/endosperm starch accumulation, grain appearance quality, and 1000-grain weight in mutant backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A deployable engineering target for increasing amylose and improving grain quality, especially when combined with upstream starch-granule-targeting factors such as OsLESV/OsESV1
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Only partial rescue of upstream mutant phenotypes, indicating dependence on additional factors for full starch restoration
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsLESV is crucial for targeting GBSSI from plastid stroma to starch granules; soluble GBSSI increases in oslesv endosperm, indicating impaired granule targeting

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycosyltransferase; starch synthase family; granule-bound starch synthase
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: oslesv-1 and osesv1-1 mutant backgrounds in Nipponbare
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream; terminal polymer elongation step on starch granule
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transitory carbon storage in leaves supporting nighttime metabolism and source-sink carbon allocation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Amylose glucan chains in leaf starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes leaf amylose/transitory starch accumulation; overexpression partially rescues low-starch phenotypes of oslesv and osesv1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction assays with OsLESV/OsESV1 (Y2H, BiFC, GST pull-down); immunoblot abundance analysis; enzyme activity assay; granule-bound protein fraction analysis; overexpression rescue in mutant backgrounds; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast starch granule; insoluble granule-bound fraction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in leaves; expression induced by carbon starvation marker context and reduced in oslesv/osesv1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq context for starch-metabolism genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for leaf amylose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GBSSII physically interacts with both OsLESV and OsESV1. 2. Its transcript, protein abundance, enzyme activity, and granule-bound fraction are reduced in oslesv, osesv1, and double mutants. 3. OsLESV and OsESV1 facilitate targeting of GBSSII to starch granules, and GBSSII overexpression partially rescues mutant phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve transitory starch formation and source strength, thereby supporting grain filling and quality
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Only partial rescue of upstream mutant phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsESV1 is particularly important for GBSSII granule targeting; increased soluble GBSSII in mutants indicates impaired starch-granule localization

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: oslesv-1 and osesv1-1 mutant backgrounds in Nipponbare
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Carbon metabolism linked to starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carbon storage in leaves and endosperm affecting grain quality and yield
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf chloroplast-associated starch pathway; developing endosperm starch biosynthetic tissues
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes reversible interconversion between pyruvate and phosphoenolpyruvate, contributing to pyruvate/hexose carbon flux that supports starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports starch accumulation in leaves and endosperm; reduced abundance and activity in oslesv/osesv1 mutants and overexpression partially rescues mutant phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction assays with OsLESV/OsESV1 (Y2H, BiFC, GST pull-down); immunoblot abundance analysis; enzyme activity assay; overexpression rescue; CRISPR mutant genetic interaction with oslesv/osesv1
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosolic, as discussed in paper; protein analyzed in leaves and developing endosperms
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in developing endosperm and leaves; reduced in oslesv/osesv1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq context for starch/carbon metabolism genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme in carbon interconversion supporting starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing endosperm and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PPDKB directly interacts with OsLESV and OsESV1. 2. Its transcript abundance, protein level, and enzyme activity are reduced in oslesv, osesv1, and double mutants. 3. PPDKB overexpression partially rescues starch, grain quality, and grain weight defects, while ppdkb combined with oslesv or osesv1 causes more severe endosperm abnormalities.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to strengthen carbon flux into starch biosynthesis, particularly in combination with starch-granule-targeting or stabilizing proteins
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Only partial rescue of upstream mutant defects; double mutants with oslesv/osesv1 show more severe wrinkled endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as a key enzyme for interconversion of pyruvate and hexose; paper emphasizes upstream support role rather than direct glucan polymerization
